metadata.documentation: With Service Quotas, you can view and manage your quotas easily as your Amazon Web Services workloads grow. Quotas, also referred to as limits, are the maximum number of resources that you can create in your Amazon Web Services account. For more information, see the Service Quotas User Guide.

AssociateServiceQuotaTemplate.documentation: Associates your quota request template with your organization. When a new Amazon Web Services account is created in your organization, the quota increase requests in the template are automatically applied to the account. You can add a quota increase request for any adjustable quota to your template.

DisassociateServiceQuotaTemplate.documentation: Disables your quota request template. After a template is disabled, the quota increase requests in the template are not applied to new Amazon Web Services accounts in your organization. Disabling a quota request template does not apply its quota increase requests.

DependencyAccessDeniedException.documentation: You can't perform this action because a dependency does not have access.

ErrorReason.documentation: An error that explains why an action did not succeed.

members.documentation:
Service Quotas returns the following error values:

  • DEPENDENCY_ACCESS_DENIED_ERROR - The caller does not have the required permissions to complete the action. To resolve the error, you must have permission to access the Amazon Web Service or quota.

  • DEPENDENCY_THROTTLING_ERROR - The Amazon Web Service is throttling Service Quotas.

  • DEPENDENCY_SERVICE_ERROR - The Amazon Web Service is not available.

  • SERVICE_QUOTA_NOT_AVAILABLE_ERROR - There was an error in Service Quotas.
